zh.Béthune, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France,: 法國豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯貝圖內
zh.lifeSummary: 當Guillaume de Béthune III於1202年出生在法國阿托斯時，他的父親Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israel48歲，母親Matilda DE TENREMONDE32歲。他和Elisabeth de Pontrohart至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1243年8月24日享年41歲。

zh-hans.Béthune, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France,: 法国豪-德-福朗斯帕-德-卡拉斯貝圖內
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Guillaume de Béthune III于1202年出生在法国阿托斯时，他的父亲Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israel48岁，母亲Matilda DE TENREMONDE32岁。他和Elisabeth de Pontrohart至少有3个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1243年8月24日享年41岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Béthune
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin); French (Béthune): habitational name from Béthune in Pas-de-Calais, Picardy, France, recorded in the 8th century in the Latin form Bitunia, probably an adjective (with villa understood) derived from the oblique form of an ancient Germanic personal name Betto, an assimilated form of Berto (see Bert ). See also Scottish Beaton .

variants.name: Beaton
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Béthune in Pas-de-Calais, France (see Bethune ). The Hebridean family of McBeth , hereditary physicians, adopted the surname Beaton in the 16th century.
English: from the Middle English personal name Beton, a diminutive of Bete, a pet form of Beatrice (see Beatrice ). Compare Beat .

variants.name: Bert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

nameMeanings.name: Guillaume
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: William
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Guillaume de Béthune III was born in 1202, in Artois, France, his father, Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israel, was 48 and his mother, Matilda DE TENREMONDE, was 32. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Elisabeth de Pontrohart. He died on 24 August 1243, at the age of 41.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Guillaume de Béthune III est né en 1202, à Artois, France, son père, Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israel, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Matilda DE TENREMONDE, avait 32 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Elisabeth de Pontrohart. Il est décédé le 24 août 1243, à l’âge de 41 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Guillaume de Béthune III 1202 in Artois, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israel 48 und seine Mutter Matilda DE TENREMONDE 32. Er hatte mit Elisabeth de Pontrohart mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 24. August 1243 im Alter von 41 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume de Béthune III è nato il nel 1202 nel Artois, Francia, il suo padre, Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israel, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Matilda DE TENREMONDE, aveva 32 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie con Elisabeth de Pontrohart. Morì il 24 agosto 1243, all'età di 41 anni.

ja.Béthune, Pas-de-Calais, Hauts-de-France,: フランスハウトス-デ-フランスプアス-デ-カレイスブeスウン
ja.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Béthune IIIが1202年にフランスアルトイスで生まれた時，父Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israelは48歳，母Matilda DE TENREMONDEは32歳であった。彼はElisabeth de Pontrohartとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1243年8月24日に41歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Guillaume de Béthune III(이)가 1202년 프랑스 아토이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israel(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda DE TENREMONDE(은)는 32세였다. 그는 Elisabeth de Pontrohart(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1243년 8월 24일 41세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Guillaume de Béthune III nasceu em 1202, em Artois, França, seu pai, Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israel, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Matilda DE TENREMONDE, tinha 32 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas com Elisabeth de Pontrohart. Ele faleceu em 24 de agosto de 1243, com 41 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Guillaume de Béthune III родился в 1202 г. в местности артоис, Франция, его отцу по имени Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israel было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda DE TENREMONDE, 32. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Elisabeth de Pontrohart. Он умер 24 августа 1243 г. в возрасте 41 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Guillaume de Béthune III nació en 1202, en Artois, Francia, su papá, Guillaume de Bethune II, Robert the Red, Crusader 1177 Israel, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Matilda DE TENREMONDE, tenía 32 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas con Elisabeth de Pontrohart. Murió el 24 de agosto de 1243, a la edad de 41 años.
